business_problem: Skyscanner handles 35 million searches and tens of billions of events every day, so a healthy unified data ecosystem is essential for travel search at this scale — teams across many use cases need to share reliable data and move quickly without breaking safety or governance.
solution_summary: Skyscanner unifies its data ecosystem on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to handle 35 million searches and tens of billions of events every day. Bronze, silver, and gold layers feed shared 'Cornerstone Data Products' that many teams reuse, including a flight ranking model that serves 180 million predictions daily. Unity Catalog governs access to the shared products so teams can move faster and experiment safely on the same trusted data, while emerging agentic AI capabilities are being layered on top to surface more relevant results for travelers across the globe.
business_outcome: With shared Cornerstone Data Products built on a bronze/silver/gold medallion architecture and governed by Unity Catalog, Skyscanner ranks flights at 180 million predictions per day, processes 35 million daily searches and tens of billions of events, and can experiment with agentic AI safely.
metrics: Tens of billions of events per day
architecture_clues: Shared 'Cornerstone Data Products' powering many use cases
evidence_quotes: Skyscanner handles 35 million searches and tens of billions of events every day
a flight ranking model serving 180 million predictions daily
By building bronze, silver and gold layers on the Databricks Platform, teams create shared Cornerstone Data Products that power many use cases

solution_components.name: Databricks Data Intelligence Platform
solution_components.role: Hosts the bronze/silver/gold medallion and shared Cornerstone Data Products powering 35M daily searches, tens of billions of events, and a flight-ranking model serving 180M predictions/day

solution_components.name: Unity Catalog
solution_components.role: Governs the shared Cornerstone Data Products across teams and underpins emerging agentic AI capabilities

data_flow: Tens of billions of events from 35 million daily Skyscanner searches land in the Databricks Lakehouse and flow through bronze, silver, and gold layers into shared 'Cornerstone Data Products' that many internal teams reuse. A flight-ranking model trained on these products serves 180 million predictions a day. Unity Catalog governs the shared data products and is the foundation for emerging agentic AI capabilities.
integration_points: Skyscanner travel-search front-ends consuming the flight-ranking model (180M predictions/day)
35M daily searches generating tens of billions of events as input
Multiple internal teams sharing Cornerstone Data Products
